projectDescription: A full-stack cybercrime complaint management platform built for the Rajasthan Police 1930 Helpline, enabling victims to file online financial fraud complaints, police to verify cases, banks to process account de-freeze requests, courts to review verdicts, and ML models to detect phishing and fraud legitimacy.
keyFeatures: Role-based dashboards for Victims, Police, Banks, and Courts
Online financial fraud complaint filing with proof upload
ML-powered phishing email detection (Logistic Regression + TF-IDF)
Bank account de-freeze request and approval flow
AI chatbot for guided complaint filing support
JWT-based authentication with role-based access control
MongoDB data storage for complaints and user accounts
publicApiSurface: POST /signup — register a new user (victim, police, bank, or court)
POST /login — authenticate and receive a session token
POST /valid — validate an existing JWT token
POST /findusertype — determine the role of the authenticated user
POST /formupload/upload — submit a new cybercrime complaint with proof
GET /formupload/downloadforms — list all complaints for the authenticated user
POST /formupload/verifybypolice — mark a complaint as verified by police
POST /formupload/defreezebybank — process a de-freeze request by bank
